channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dar es Salaam Set to Host Major African Agricultural Business Expo in 2026
item.description: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania, is set to host the 10th Jubilee edition of the Africa Agri Expo on September 2–3, 2026, together with the 3rd Future Food Livestock & Poultry Expo. After nine successful editi...

item.title: Bangladeshi Man Sentenced in UP for Using Fake Indian Passport and Illegal Land Registration
item.description: A Bangladeshi national, Abdul Amin, has been sentenced to three years imprisonment by a court in Ballia, Uttar Pradesh, for travelling on a forged Indian passport and illegally registering land in Wes...

item.title: Apolosi Ranawai's inspiring journey from personal tragedy and 170kg to Super Rugby debut with the NSW Waratahs
item.description: NSW Waratahs prop Apolosi Ranawai has shared his remarkable story of resilience after making his Super Rugby debut at the age of 32. Just two years ago, following multiple family tragedies in Fiji inc...

item.title: New Mayors Elected in Fredericton and Moncton During New Brunswick Municipal Elections
item.description: Two of New Brunswick's largest cities have recently elected new mayors, according to unofficial results from Elections New Brunswick. In Fredericton, Steve Hicks, a long-serving city councillor, won t...

item.title: Thieves Steal Front Grille from UN Vehicle in Nairobi Traffic Jam
item.description: In Nairobi, a shocking theft has stirred concern over growing insecurity on the city's roads. On Monday afternoon, around 3:15 PM, three suspected criminals managed to steal the front grille of a Unit...

item.title: Uzodimma reports foreign reserves rise to $49.4bn following Tinubu’s economic reforms
item.description: Senator Hope Uzodimma, Governor of Imo State and Chairman of the Progressive Governors’ Forum, don yan say the economic reforms under President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s Renewed Hope agenda don really boost...

item.title: Pakistan rejects claims of Iranian military aircraft at Nur Khan Airbase, clarifies role in diplomatic facilitation
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) of Pakistan has strongly rejected claims made by an American media outlet that Islamabad allowed Iranian military aircraft to park at its airfields while acting as a mediator i...

item.title: Hantavirus Cases Confirmed Among Cruise Passengers; Spanish and French Patients Isolated
item.description: Authorities have confirmed a new case of hantavirus among passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship. A Spanish national, one of 14 quarantined at a military hospital in Madrid, tested posit...

item.title: EKN Support Strengthens Investor Confidence in Tanzania’s SGR Expansion
item.description: Sweden’s Export Credit Agency (EKN) has explained how its involvement in financing Tanzania’s Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) is helping to attract more investors and strengthen the project’s credibility...

item.title: Exploring Tranquil Retreats in Franschhoek: La Cotte and Rickety Bridge
item.description: Franschhoek continues to charm visitors with its mix of art, food, wine, and scenic beauty. While infrastructure upgrades like the R45 road expansion are underway to handle growing tourist traffic, so...

item.title: NCDC reports 190 deaths from Lassa fever in 2026, with rising fatality rate
item.description: The Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) don report say 190 people don die from Lassa fever so far for 2026. According to the latest Lassa Fever Situation Report covering 20 to 26 ...

item.title: Access Issues Prevent Reading Heidelberg Weather Update
item.description: The content you attempted to access from News Corp Australia's Herald Sun website regarding today's weather in Heidelberg was blocked due to automated crawler detection. News Corp uses specialised sof...

item.title: BoP and Flat Owners File Appeals Against IHC Ruling on One Constitution Avenue Lease
item.description: In Islamabad, the Bank of Punjab (BoP) along with several flat owners of One Constitution Avenue have filed separate intra-court appeals before the Islamabad High Court (IHC) challenging a single benc...

item.title: President Samia Hassan to Attend Museveni Swearing-in Ceremony in Kampala
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan is set to travel to Kampala, Uganda today on May 12, 2026, to attend the inauguration ceremony of Uganda's President Yoweri Kaguta Museveni. According to the official sta...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Maintains Central Bank Rate at 5.75% for Second Quarter of 2026 Amid Strong Economic Performance
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania (BOT) has decided to keep the Central Bank Rate (CBR) unchanged at 5.75 percent for the second quarter of 2026. This cautious approach comes as the Monetary Policy Committee balan...

item.title: High Court Awards Compensation to Student Injured in Road Accident
item.description: On May 12, 2026, Kenyan newspapers reported that the High Court awarded KSh 623,015 to a young learner identified as EK, who sustained severe injuries including skull fractures, deep cuts, and chest c...

item.title: JOOUST Student Alice Riang’a Found Dead, Boyfriend Shares Emotional Tribute
item.description: Alice Riang’a, a second-year student at Jaramogi Oginga Odinga University of Science and Technology (JOOUST) pursuing a Bachelor of Education in Science (Mathematics and Chemistry), was tragically fou...

item.title: Race Could Continue Influencing Partisan Redistricting in the U.S.
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court recently invalidated Louisiana’s majority-Black congressional district in a 6-3 decision, ruling it an unconstitutional racial gerrymander. This ruling has raised concerns among...

item.title: Analysing Keir Starmer’s Challenges as Labour Leader After Election Setback
item.description: In the wake of one of the worst election results in Labour’s history, Keir Starmer faced a difficult task addressing the nation. His leadership has attracted widespread scrutiny, with many questioning...

item.title: Himanta Biswa Sarma sworn in as Assam Chief Minister for second term, four MLAs take oath as Ministers
item.description: Himanta Biswa Sarma has been sworn in as the Chief Minister of Assam for his second consecutive term on 12th May 2026. The oath-taking ceremony was held at the Veterinary Ground in Khanapara, Guwahati...
